— video Meliora probóque , deteriora sequor — hence the whole soul be fill with fierce contradition and conflict . vanity , instability , folly , sensual irrational appetite , inordinate desire , self-disquieting and torture passion , act continual in our deprave nature . see the account hereof , Rom. 3. 10 , 11 , 12 , 13 , 14 , 15 , 16 , 17 , 18. how full be the world of disorder , confusion , oppression , rapine , uncleanness , violence , and the like dreadful misery ? Alas ! what be the perish profit , pleasure , and satisfaction by they , which this world can afford ? how unable be the mind of man to find out rest and peace in they or from they ? they quick satiate and suffocate in their enjoyment , and become to have no relish in their variety , which only heighten present vanity , and treasure up provision for future vexation . we have therefore no great interest in the world , than to inquire how this disorder may be cure , and a stop put to this fountain of all abomination . what we intend , will be clear in the ensue observation . that light . more particular , there be three main way of abuse this liberty , or the flesh be very apt to take occasion by this liberty to corrupt it self and prejudice the life of its spirit , chief in these three respect : in reference to its temper , in reference to its rest , in reference to its motion . what the spirit slay by true spiritual liberty , the flesh get into the outside of it Xiii . 1. that Christ be no blasphemer in declare himself to be the son of God ; 2. nor conjurer in cast out devil . 3. that he be unjust accuse of profaneness . 4. that there be nothing detestable in his neutrality towards political faction : 5. nor any injustice nor partiality find in he . 6. nor can his sharp rebuke of the Pharisee be right term rail ; 7. nor his whip the buyer and seller out of the temple tumultuary zeal ; 8. nor his cry out so dreadful in his passion be impute to impatience or despair . 9 the suspicion of distractedness and madness clear . 10. his vindication from their aspersion of looseness and prodigality . 11. the crooked and perverse nature of the Pharisee note ; with our saviour own apology for his frequent all company . 12. that Christ be no self-seek in undergo the death of the cross for that joy that be set before he . 1. the former part of which task though it may seem needless , if not ridiculous , among Christians , who can entertain any evil thought of that person who they deserve worship ; yet because all that live in christian commonwealth be not cordial such in no manner at all ; for the convince of they , if it be possible , of the excellency of Christ , or at least for the better stop of vain mouth from rash and unskilful censure , i hold it not improper to recite to you a charge or bill of indictment exhibit against that innocent and immaculate lamb Christ Jesus by malicious and ignorant man ; to the intent that he , who they have so unworthy charge , may be as honourable dismiss and acquit , that his righteousness may be bring forth as the morning , Psal. 37.6 . and his judgement as the noon day . and here that they may fly high enough at first , and strike deep enough even to a deserve take away of life , blasphemy must stand in the front , to give countenance and strength to the rest of their follow accusation . 2. then conjure and deal with the devil . 3. profanation of the sabbath . 4. neutrality , or cold indifferency in public controversy . 5. injustice . 6. rail . 7. tumultuary and injurious zeal . 7. impatience and despair . 9 frenzy or madness . 10. debauchery and looseness of life . 11. lavishment and prodigality . 12. and last , ambition and self-interest . these be the several dunghill from whence wicked and perverse man will industrious dig out dirt to cast in the face of he who be the perfect pattern of divine purity and righteousness . but let they ply themselves as fast as they can in these several foul pit , it will not be hard to find wherewith to wipe it off as fast as their impious diligence shall be able to cast it on . and first let we consider what work they make in the first place as concern blasphemy . John 10. for declare God his father , and that he and his father be one , he be there furious accuse of blasphemy , and ready to be stone . and John 8. they be also there ready to stone he for say he be before Abraham . and Matth. 9.3 . he be there also accuse of blasphemy for say , son , be of good cheer , thy sin be forgive thou . and here in this first accusation de facto constat ; Christ confess , that he be one with God , that he be the son of God , and that he have power to forgive sin , that he be before Abraham : but it be utter deny , that in any of all this Christ do blaspheme . for first , consider the very word of Christ , i and my father be one . how unreasonable and inconsequent do these dull and peevish perverter of the word of he who they so entire hate for his good life and doctrine , deduce from that say , i and my father be one , that he , be a man , make himself God ? for it be as childish and inept infer from thence by they , that he make himself God ; as if they shall conclude , that the body be the very soul , because the body and the soul be one , that be , one man . and it be no more falsehood , much less blasphemy , for the humanity of Christ ( who be so real and lively actuate , inform , and unite with God , as the body be with the soul ) to pronounce of himself as if he be very God , than it be for the tongue to say , i understand , i believe , i perceive , when neither the body nor it believe , perceive , or understand any thing , but only the soul with which it be so intimate unite , and of which that which the tongue speak in such case be to be understand . and if this be due consider and take in , Christ say also , that he be before Abraham , will not prove any blasphemy . for Christ by reason of he so near union and essential conjunction with God , which Athanasius well resemble to that of the body and soul , may as proper and natural profess himself to be before Abraham , yea , to have be before the world be make , as the tongue of man may utter , i shall survive after the death of this my body ; in which there be no ill sense nor incongruity in the judgement of most sober man . but beside this , that which the blind Jew understand not , be hoodwink with the thickness of their own particular religion and unregenerate nature , sometime Christ speak of himself under the notion of a divine life and unction communicable to the son of man in all age and place . wherefore the sense be this , that whereas the Jew religion and topical and temporary holiness begin but as high as from Abraham ; that of Christ , which he exhibit in that fullness to the world , be true universal both for time as well as place ; a light that enlighten every man that come into the world ; the eternal wisdom of God , that in all age make those that receive it friend of God and prophet , Wisd. 7.27 . as the wise man speak . and it be no wonder that divine man accord to their high or more intimate union with the divinity lose their sense and remembrance of their particularity , and pronounce of themselves rather accord to the thing they be so live unite with , then accord to their own vanish circumscribe corporeal person . and now it be no blasphemy , as be plain , to admit that one may be thus lively actuate by and unite to God , in who , if any where , the mind of God must dwell ; who can more reasonable remit sin then such a one and so manifest a prophet as our saviour declare himself by his sign and miracle do among the people ? but our saviour have so excellent answer for himself , and so apposite , as to the condition of his opposer , that when i have rehearse it , this first accusation will be more than satisfy . Joh. 10.34 , 35. be it not write in your law , i say , you be god ? if he call they god unto who the word of God come , and the scripture can be break ; say you of he who the father have sanctify , and send into the world , thou blaspheme ; because i say , i be the son of God ? if i do not the work of my father , believe i not . but if i do , though you believe not i , believe the work ; that you may know and believe that the father be in i and i in he . and sure at the very first sight this be a right sober plea to any unprejudiced judge , that our saviour be so far from a blasphemer , that for his life he be a saint , or rather the pattern and original of all saintship , for his miracle the power of God , and for his nature and relation filius dei , the son of God , in a very safe scriptural and judaical sense ; to trouble their low apprehension with no high nor hard conception . which conception be notwithstanding not so hard as true : and the write of the apostle and the evangelist be judge ( to who every Christian be bind to appeal ) i conceive it will easy appear to indifferent man , that the godhead belong to Christ real and essential , not titular , be as necessary include in the formalis ratio of his nature , as three angle in the notion of a triangle . and in my own judgement , i can acquit those man who be so busy against the divinity of Christ ( whenas yet they will be call and esteem Christians ) from be guilty not only of high indiscretion , but of a very grand error in Christianity . but the Jew , to who this great mystery of the coalition of God and man into one person be not then reveal , do very perverse to interpret Christ word into such a sense as they may with confidence call blasphemy ; whenas they may have interpret they accord to what be more compliable with the tenor of their own faith . in this , i say , be their malice very remarkable , that they will not afford his say a ordinary benign interpretation , who work and action be so miraculous and divine , and his life so full of goodness and innocency . 2. but such be the perverseness of this stupid nation , that even those thing that shall have wrought a acknowledgement of their Messiah , make they more obstinate ; and they must be less his friend , because he be a foe to the devil , and deem he a conspirator against God , when it be his business to dislodge Satan wherever he find he .
